selection_basis: Five mandatory architecture fine-tuning runs are complete.
Phase 7C 100-image inference benchmark is complete for all five candidates.
Selection used measured COCO-style quality, measured image inference speed, measured checkpoint size, integration practicality, dependency risk, expected video suitability, and known limitations.
COCO quality metrics and push-up video/demo stability remain separate evaluation topics.

quality_summary.interpretation: RTMPose-S does not have the highest recorded AP; HRNet-W32 UDP is higher at 0.786985. RTMPose-S is selected because its quality is adequate for the demo while providing a much better practical speed/size balance.
quality_summary.metric_caution: YOLO Pose pose_mAP50-95 and MMPose coco/AP are recorded as reported by their tools and are not treated as identical metrics.

speed_summary.interpretation: RTMPose-S was the fastest model in the Phase 7C image benchmark. This is not a real video benchmark and is not used to claim real-time video performance.

size_summary.interpretation: RTMPose-S is larger than YOLO Pose and Lite-HRNet-18 but much smaller than HRNet-W32 UDP and ViTPose-Small Simple Head, making it practical for the final demo.

integration_summary.dependency_risk: MMPose requires the separate project MMPose environment, but it has already been used successfully for training and benchmarking.
integration_summary.demo_scope: The demo app must use only this selected model and must not expose a model-selection dropdown.

video_suitability_summary.status: expected suitable for selected-model video evaluation
video_suitability_summary.basis: Fastest measured image inference among the five candidates with acceptable keypoint AP and moderate checkpoint size.

limitations: Final decision is based on image quality metrics and 100-image image inference speed, not a real video benchmark.
Push-up-specific video stability metrics are not measured yet.
Successful and failed push-up examples are not collected yet.
Typical errors are not analyzed yet and must be collected in Phase 9.
The training/evaluation dataset is a resource-limited COCO Keypoints subset, not full COCO and not a manually annotated push-up dataset.

not_selected_reasons_by_model.yolo_pose: Small and fast, with simple Ultralytics integration, but its recorded pose_mAP50-95 is lower in this experiment and the metric is not directly equivalent to MMPose coco/AP.
not_selected_reasons_by_model.hrnet_w32_udp: Highest recorded MMPose AP, but much slower in the Phase 7C benchmark and the largest checkpoint, making it less practical for the demo.
not_selected_reasons_by_model.litehrnet18: Smallest checkpoint, but it had the slowest measured mean inference time in Phase 7C and the lowest recorded MMPose AP.
not_selected_reasons_by_model.vitpose_small_simple: Fast enough and slightly higher AP than RTMPose-S, but substantially larger and has higher dependency complexity due to the transformer/MMPose stack.

required_next_steps: Integrate RTMPose-S as the only demo model in Phase 8.
Run selected-model video-oriented evaluation before making video performance claims.
Create outputs/metrics/video_benchmark.csv only after a real video benchmark.
Collect at least 3 successful and 3 failed push-up examples in Phase 9.
Collect observed typical errors in Phase 9 instead of inventing them.
